title: ONVIF IP Camera RTSP Session Disconnect Burst
dataSources: `index=physical` VMS/NVR diagnostic logs with `sourcetype=onvif:events` or `sourcetype=nvr:rtsp` containing `Stream`, `Teardown`, `401`, `404` tokens
app: Milestone XProtect / Genetec / Axis AVHS forwarded syslog, or Universal Forwarder monitoring NVR application logs
spl:
index=physical (sourcetype=onvif:events OR sourcetype=nvr:rtsp OR sourcetype=hikvision:isapi)
| search RTSP OR rtsp OR Stream OR teardown OR Disconnect OR 454
| bin _time span=5m
| stats count as drops by _time, host, camera_id
| where drops > 30
description: Surveillance clients reopen RTSP sessions constantly; a burst of teardowns or `454 Session Not Found` errors often precedes camera power loss, firmware faults, or switch PoE budget exhaustion. Counting drops per camera distinguishes flaky cameras from single NVR restarts.
value: Restores evidentiary continuity by catching video transport failures before operators notice black tiles on the wall.
implementation: Normalize `camera_id` from ONVIF `ReferenceToken` or NVR channel GUID. Ingest NVR logs via syslog with `sourcetype=nvr:rtsp` or reuse vendor add-on sourcetypes. Tune `drops > 30` to camera bitrate and keyframe interval. Correlate with PoE `switch` SNMP if available.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Time sync between cameras, NVR, Splunk.
• Channel-to-asset lookup.

Step 1 — Sample logs during camera reboot.

Step 2 — Exclude scheduled VMS upgrades.

Step 3 — Correlate with access control for tamper hypothesis.

Step 4 — Retention per legal hold policy.
grandmaExplanation: We count how often the video stream drops for each camera so security teams notice failing feeds before an incident happens.
